content:
I wonder if there's any plans to somehow have the syn and quote crates available so that it's easier to write proc macros?

That..would be very useful.
It's been looked at before. Doing _any_ 3p crate in the kernel will require us to do some foundational work first though.
Yeah, I just noticed there's prior discussion by @Vincenzo Palazzo
There is #910, but this will probably still be a long way until we have it.

I am putting some effort into it without required a full library like syn at least for the initial phase, and this is the library that I am writing https://github.com/vincenzopalazzo/kproc-macros

In addition, I also make an initial integration here https://git.hedwing.dev/vincenzopalazzo/linux/src/branch/macros/kparserv2 but I gues this branch did not contains the last version of the library.

At the curremt moment I am iteratin over the API with self usage of the library, but it you will have some feedback on it, I am pretty happy to receive feedbacks
There is the quote! macro in the builtin proc_macro crate, unstable but still https://doc.rust-lang.org/proc_macro/macro.quote.html
Of course doesn't help with syn or proc_macro2, but it's half the battle

Trevor Gross said:

There is the quote! macro in the builtin proc_macro crate, unstable but still https://doc.rust-lang.org/proc_macro/macro.quote.html

Right, but this can be used on top of the kproc-macros and use have a minimal free dependecies procedural macros parser

Trevor Gross said:

There is the quote! macro in the builtin proc_macro crate, unstable but still https://doc.rust-lang.org/proc_macro/macro.quote.html

I was missing this btw, so I can ping some guys from rust side to get see if there is any idea on how go forwards with it, thanks to point it out
